title: Bank of Canada Webcasts Listing Page
summary: This is a calendar/index listing page from the Bank of Canada website showing scheduled and past webcasts of speeches and press conferences. The page contains no substantive macro catalyst content—it lists only event titles, dates, and times. Actual policy-relevant information would only be available by accessing individual event links, which are not included in this source. This page has no standalone archival value.
key_points: Page is a webcast listing/calendar, not a source of macro catalyst content
Lists Bank of Canada Governor and Deputy Governor appearances from December 2025 to March 2026
Includes scheduled events such as Policy Rate Announcements and Monetary Policy Report releases
Event titles reference topics including monetary policy, financial stability, and structural change
No substantive policy content, statements, or data present in this source
The URL field is blank, limiting source traceability
why_it_matters: While the Bank of Canada is a relevant macro catalyst source and the listed event types (e.g., Policy Rate Announcements, Monetary Policy Reports) would be highly relevant if actual content were present, this specific page provides no actionable macro information. It serves only as a navigation aid. Per Rule 8, listing pages must not be archived.

issues_found: Source is a listing/calendar page (webcasts index), prohibited by Rule 8
No substantive macro catalyst content present—only event titles and dates
URL is blank, preventing source verification and traceability
No actual policy statements, economic data, or market-relevant content
Source is a container page with no standalone archival value
PAGE_TYPE should be 'listing_page', not 'article' or content page

notes: SOURCE IS NON-ARCHIVABLE: This is a listing/calendar page (webcasts index) containing no substantive macro catalyst content. The page lists event names and dates but provides no actual policy statements, economic data, or market-relevant content. The target topic is 'macro catalysts' and the listed events (rate announcements, speeches) would be relevant if the actual event content were present, but this source is merely an index. Confidence is extremely low for any market-relevant extraction. Per Rule 9: listing pages, navigation pages, and container pages should be treated as non-archiveable.
